Package neqsim.pvtsimulation.util

Class WaterPropertyCorrelations

java.lang.Object
neqsim.pvtsimulation.util.WaterPropertyCorrelations
public final class WaterPropertyCorrelations extends Object
Water and brine property correlations for reservoir engineering.

Provides empirical correlations for water/brine thermodynamic and transport properties commonly needed in reservoir simulation and production engineering. All public methods use Kelvin for temperature and bara for pressure by default.

Default units: temperature in Kelvin (K), pressure in bara, salinity in ppm (mg/L) unless otherwise noted. Correlations are computed internally in their published unit systems (typically Fahrenheit / psia) and converted at the API boundary.

Correlations included:

  • Water FVF - McCain (1991) and Osif (1988)
  • Water Viscosity - McCain and Meehan (1980) dead + live water
  • Water Compressibility - McCain (1991) with salinity correction
  • Brine Density - Batzle and Wang (1992)
  • Solution Gas-Water Ratio - Culberson and McKetta (1951)
  • Water-Gas Surface Tension - Jennings and Newman (1971)
Version:
2.0
Author:
ESOL

  • Method Details

    • toF

      private static double toF(double tempK)
      Convert Kelvin to Fahrenheit.
      Parameters:
      tempK - Temperature in Kelvin
      Returns:
      Temperature in Fahrenheit

    • toPsia

      private static double toPsia(double pBara)
      Convert bara to psia.
      Parameters:
      pBara - Pressure in bara
      Returns:
      Pressure in psia

    • waterFVFMcCain

      public static double waterFVFMcCain(double temperatureK, double pressureBara)
      McCain (1991) water formation volume factor.

      Calculates Bw (res bbl/STB) for pure water (no salinity correction). Internally uses Fahrenheit/psia coefficients. $$ B_w = (1 + \Delta V_{wT})(1 + \Delta V_{wP}) $$

      Parameters:
      temperatureK - Temperature (Kelvin). Valid: 275 - 475 K
      pressureBara - Pressure (bara). Valid: 1 - 350 bara
      Returns:
      Water formation volume factor (res bbl/STB), dimensionless

    • waterFVFOsif

      public static double waterFVFOsif(double temperatureK, double pressureBara, double salinityPpm)
      Osif (1988) water formation volume factor for brine.

      Includes salinity effects on water FVF. $$ B_w = \left[1 + \frac{a_1 (T-60) + a_2 (T-60)^2 - a_3 P - a_4 P^2 + a_5 P (T-60)}{a_6}\right] $$

      Parameters:
      temperatureK - Temperature (Kelvin). Valid: 275 - 475 K
      pressureBara - Pressure (bara). Valid: 1 - 350 bara
      salinityPpm - Total dissolved solids (ppm NaCl equivalent)
      Returns:
      Water formation volume factor (res bbl/STB), dimensionless

    • deadWaterViscosityMcCain

      public static double deadWaterViscosityMcCain(double temperatureK, double salinityPpm)
      McCain dead water viscosity (no dissolved gas).

      Based on Meehan (1980) correlation. Internally uses Fahrenheit. $$ \mu_{w,dead} = A T^B $$

      Parameters:
      temperatureK - Temperature (Kelvin). Valid: 275 - 475 K
      salinityPpm - Total dissolved solids (ppm NaCl equivalent)
      Returns:
      Dead water viscosity (cP)

    • waterViscosityMcCain

      public static double waterViscosityMcCain(double temperatureK, double pressureBara, double salinityPpm)
      McCain water viscosity with dissolved gas correction.

      Applies Meehan (1980) pressure correction to the dead water viscosity. $$ \mu_w = \mu_{w,dead} \times [0.9994 + 4.0295 \times 10^{-5} P + 3.1062 \times 10^{-9} P^2] $$

      Parameters:
      temperatureK - Temperature (Kelvin)
      pressureBara - Pressure (bara)
      salinityPpm - Total dissolved solids (ppm NaCl equivalent)
      Returns:
      Water viscosity at P and T (cP)

    • waterCompressibilityMcCain

      public static double waterCompressibilityMcCain(double temperatureK, double pressureBara, double salinityPpm)
      McCain (1991) water compressibility.

      Calculates isothermal compressibility of water or brine. Returns result in 1/bara. Internal calculation in 1/psi, converted to 1/bara at the boundary.

      Parameters:
      temperatureK - Temperature (Kelvin). Valid: 275 - 450 K
      pressureBara - Pressure (bara). Valid: 7 - 350 bara
      salinityPpm - Total dissolved solids (ppm NaCl equivalent)
      Returns:
      Water compressibility (1/bara)

    • brineDensityBatzleWang

      public static double brineDensityBatzleWang(double temperatureK, double pressureBara, double salinityWtFrac)
      Batzle and Wang (1992) brine density correlation.

      Calculates brine density from temperature and pressure. Uses the Batzle-Wang formulation with temperature in Celsius and pressure in MPa internally.

      Parameters:
      temperatureK - Temperature (Kelvin). Valid: 275 - 475 K
      pressureBara - Pressure (bara). Valid: 1 - 1000 bara
      salinityWtFrac - Salinity as weight fraction [0, 0.3]
      Returns:
      Brine density (kg/m3)

    • waterDensity

      public static double waterDensity(double temperatureK, double pressureBara)
      Simple water density using Batzle-Wang with zero salinity.
      Parameters:
      temperatureK - Temperature (Kelvin)
      pressureBara - Pressure (bara)
      Returns:
      Water density (kg/m3)

    • solutionGasWaterRatioCulberson

      public static double solutionGasWaterRatioCulberson(double temperatureK, double pressureBara, double salinityPpm)
      Culberson and McKetta (1951) solution gas-water ratio.

      Estimates the volume of gas dissolved in water at reservoir conditions. Returns Rsw in Sm3/Sm3 (standard cubic metres of gas per standard cubic metre of water).

      Parameters:
      temperatureK - Temperature (Kelvin). Valid: 290 - 510 K
      pressureBara - Pressure (bara). Valid: 7 - 700 bara
      salinityPpm - Total dissolved solids (ppm NaCl equivalent)
      Returns:
      Solution gas-water ratio (Sm3/Sm3)

    • waterGasSurfaceTension

      public static double waterGasSurfaceTension(double temperatureK, double pressureBara)
      Jennings and Newman (1971) water-gas surface tension.

      Estimates the interfacial tension (IFT) between water and gas. Pressure correction applied above 74 bara (approximately 1000 psig).

      Parameters:
      temperatureK - Temperature (Kelvin). Valid: 290 - 450 K
      pressureBara - Pressure (bara). Valid: 1 - 700 bara
      Returns:
      Surface tension (dyne/cm = mN/m)

    • waterPropertiesSummary

      public static Map<String,Double> waterPropertiesSummary(double temperatureK, double pressureBara, double salinityPpm)
      Compute a summary of water/brine properties at given conditions.
      Parameters:
      temperatureK - Temperature (Kelvin)
      pressureBara - Pressure (bara)
      salinityPpm - Total dissolved solids (ppm NaCl equivalent)
      Returns:
      Map of property name to value (SI units)
